But what's fascinating about that movie, of course, is that what happened to that movie is really happens in our lives.
We always talk about campaigns and campaigners.
And there are some people who are wonderful at campaigning.
But once you win, then you have to do something with that, all that campaigning - and that is to govern.
And some people are just so much better at campaigning than they are at governing.

Email from Anna(ph) in Austin, Texas.
I love the film ""Election"" with Reese Witherspoon as Tracy Flick, a high school uber-achiever, and Matthew Broderick as her skeptical teacher.
The satire of the film works on so many levels, so much so, that in 2008, the news website, Slate, published an article with the title ""Hillary Clinton is Tracy Flick.

It was like apples and oranges.
I had to work a little harder, that's all.
You see, I believe in the voters.
They understand that elections aren't just popularity contests.

Listen, one of my favorite films of all time is ""Seven Days in May"" for the following reason.
Kirk Douglas, you know, a colonel, gets wind of Burt Lancaster and Joint Chiefs of Staff is going to overthrow the government.
The novel co-written by a former editor here, Chuck Bailey, yeah.
Yeah.
And my interest in that was, I believe symbolically Kirk Douglas represented the press in its investigative role, even though he was military man.
I could see this as a Washington movie.
Was there an election here?
Well, there were there was a big dispute between the president and the Joint Chiefs of Staff over the missile treaty that the president had signed in the film.
But the election merely elects speaker heads that then go to work behind the scenes by and large.
So I'm more interested in what goes on behind the scenes than what happens on Election Day, which, to me, is about one percent of what's actually going on.
But I thought it was an interesting thing, in the way power actually is wielded - not so much by the public, but by people who are ostensibly elected, but then don't follow the mandate of the public.
